design_philosophy.core_loop: Issue movement commands to unit types on a map grid, position units into tactical formations, resolve turn-based combat encounters determined by terrain and unit matchups, advance toward objective (enemy HQ capture or unit elimination)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Asymmetric resource constraint: player receives fixed unit count per scenario with no production; must strategically allocate limited forces against CPU opponent who receives numerical superiority on harder difficulties
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command Allied generals (Patton/Montgomery) in North African WWII campaign; experience tactical battlefield command where terrain mastery and unit composition matter
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces terrain-dependent tactics as core mechanic—movement, attack, and defense vary by terrain type, making positioning mandatory rather than optional. Turn-based pacing emphasizes deliberation over reflexes. Asymmetric AI scaling (CPU gets more units, not better strategy) forces adaptation to quantity disadvantage rather than difficulty through smarter opponents.

difficulty.checkpointing: Per-scenario checkpoint system; five increasingly difficult scenarios with predetermined unit allocations
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players customize unit composition within scenario limits; can use second controller to modify CPU unit counts in single-player mode to adjust challenge; five difficulty tiers built into campaign structure

progression.upgrade_system: Unit replenishment system: aircraft refuel at airports, ground units resupply at designated buildings or via supply trucks; transport units carry infantry across map. No permanent upgrades between scenarios.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential scenario progression; each of five scenarios increases CPU unit allocation, creating escalating strategic challenges
replayability_hooks: Two-player versus mode with customizable unit counts for balanced play
Variable unit composition selection within per-scenario limits enables different tactical approaches
Terrain mastery rewards repeated play as players learn optimal positioning strategies

world_building_techniques: Named generals (Patton, Montgomery, Rommel) establish faction identity
Synopsis provided in manual describing broader geopolitical context (Hitler's invasion, Mussolini's Mediterranean ambitions, British defense of Egypt)

faction_indicators: Japanese version: player explicitly commands Afrika Korps as Rommel against Montgomery; Hitler appears on victory screen praising player
North American version: factions unnamed, conflict opaque—apparent content localization to avoid explicit Axis alignment for Western market
danger_signals: Explicit Nazi framing in Japanese version creates potential ideological controversy; North American sanitization suggests publisher awareness of sensitivity
environmental_storytelling: Headquarters units serve as objective markers indicating faction bases
Terrain types (varied across five campaign maps) imply North African desert geography
Military infrastructure (airports, supply buildings) reinforce WWII logistics simulation
